---
title: "AB Testing"
toc: false
---

Articles on experimentation design, statistical analysis, and simulation-driven decision making.

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### [AB Testing is an Optimization Problem](ab-testing-optimization.qmd)
**June 2026** &nbsp;·&nbsp; Decision Theory · Bayesian · Optimization

Frequentist stopping rules are not derived from any business objective. Reframes AB testing as a decision optimization problem and shows how Bayesian methods align stopping criteria with what actually matters.

[Read →](ab-testing-optimization.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### [The Winner's Curse](winners-curse.qmd)
**Jan 2026** &nbsp;·&nbsp; Bias · Bayesian

Statistical significance is not an endorsement of the treatment effect. Quantifies how often significant results overestimate true lift.

[Read →](winners-curse.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### [Beta vs Binomial Distribution](beta-vs-binomial.qmd)
**Dec 2025** &nbsp;·&nbsp; Distributions · Bayesian

When to use each distribution in experimentation. Covers p-values, credible intervals, and the Normal approximation — all built with simulations.

[Read →](beta-vs-binomial.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### [The Value of Big Swings](big-swings.qmd)
**Aug 2025** &nbsp;·&nbsp; Strategy · Simulation

Why bold experiments deliver more value with less traffic. Compares guaranteed small wins vs. high-variance transformational bets using 30,000 simulations.

[Read →](big-swings.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### [Experiment Design & Analysis with NumPy](numpy-experiment-design.qmd)
**Feb 2025** &nbsp;·&nbsp; Simulation · Power

Understanding p-values and statistical power through simulation. Builds a full AB test framework from scratch using `numpy.random`.

[Read →](numpy-experiment-design.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### [The Power of Data Simulation](power-of-simulation.qmd)
**Nov 2024** &nbsp;·&nbsp; Simulation · Intuition

Using Python's NumPy random generator to model randomness and build intuition for rate-based metrics. A foundation for all simulation-based work.

[Read →](power-of-simulation.qmd)
:::

::::
